passages.passage_text:
Directions (Q. Nos. 1-6) In the following questions, the symbols, ©, %, $ and @ are used with the following meaning as illustrated below.
P © 0 means P is either smaller than or equal to Q.
P % Q means P is smaller than Q.
P @ Q means P is equal to Q.
P * Q means P is either greater than or equal to Q.
P $ Q means P is greater than Q.
Now, in each of the following questions, assuming the given statements to be true, find which of the two Conclusions I and II given below them is/are definitely true?
Give answer
(a) if only Conclusion I is true
(b) if only Conclusion II is true
(c) if either Conclusion I or II is true
(d) if neither Conclusion I nor II is true
(e) if both Conclusion I and II are true

Directions (Q. Nos. 7-11) In the following questions, the symbols @, $, #, © and % are used with the following meaning as illustrated below.
'P $ Q' means 'P is not smaller than Q'.
'P # Q' means 'P is neither smaller than nor equal to Q'.
'P @ Q' means 'P is neither greater than nor smaller than Q'.
'P © Q' means 'P is neither greater than nor equal to Q'.
'P % Q' means 'P is not greater than Q.'
Now, in each of the following questions, assuming the given statements to be true, find which of the four Conclusions I, II, III and IV given below them is/are definitely true and give your answer accordingly.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements M % T, T $ K, K © D
Conclusions I. T $ D    II. D $ M

choices.a: if only Conclusion I is true
choices.b: if only Conclusion II is true
choices.c: if either Conclusion I or II is true
choices.d: if neither Conclusion I nor II is true
choices.e: if both Conclusion I and II are true

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements F @ B, B % N, N $ H
Conclusions I. N $ F    II. H $ F

choices.a: if only Conclusion I is true
choices.b: if only Conclusion II is true
choices.c: if either Conclusion I or II is true
choices.d: if neither Conclusion I nor II is true
choices.e: if both Conclusion I and II are true

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements R * M, M @ K, K © J
Conclusions I. J $ M    II. J @ M

choices.a: if only Conclusion I is true
choices.b: if only Conclusion II is true
choices.c: if either Conclusion I or II is true
choices.d: if neither Conclusion I nor II is true
choices.e: if both Conclusion I and II are true

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements B $ N, N * R, R @ K
Conclusions I. K © N    II. B $ K

choices.a: if only Conclusion I is true
choices.b: if only Conclusion II is true
choices.c: if either Conclusion I or II is true
choices.d: if neither Conclusion I nor II is true
choices.e: if both Conclusion I and II are true

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements J © K, K $ N, N * D
Conclusions I. J % N    II. D % K

choices.a: if only Conclusion I is true
choices.b: if only Conclusion II is true
choices.c: if either Conclusion I or II is true
choices.d: if neither Conclusion I nor II is true
choices.e: if both Conclusion I and II are true

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements R @ D, D © M, M $ T
Conclusions I. T % D    II. M * R

choices.a: if only Conclusion I is true
choices.b: if only Conclusion II is true
choices.c: if either Conclusion I or II is true
choices.d: if neither Conclusion I nor II is true
choices.e: if both Conclusion I and II are true

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements R # J, J $ D, D @ K, K % T
Conclusions I. T # D    II. T @ D
          III. J $ T    IV. J $ T

choices.a: Either I or II is true
choices.d: Either I or II and III are true

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements T % R, R $ M, M @ D, D © H
Conclusions I. D % R    II. H # R
          III. T © M    IV. T % D

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements M @ B, B # N, N $ R, R © K
Conclusions I. K # B    II. R © B
          III. M $ R    IV. N © M

choices.c: I, III and IV are true
choices.d: II, III and IV are true

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements F # H, H @ M, M © E, E $ J
Conclusions I. J © M    II. E # H
          III. M © F    IV. F # E

choices.c: I, II and III are true
choices.d: II, III and IV are true

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements D % A, A @ B, B © K, K % M
Conclusions I. B $ D    II. K # A
          III. M # B    IV. A © M

choices.a: I, II and IV are true
choices.b: I, II and III are true
choices.c: II, III and IV are true
choices.d: I, III and IV are true
